title: Package Layout, Pyproject Metadata, Entry Points, and Reproducible Builds · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved Bookbot run contains one plausible path, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, reporting, CLI, testing, performance, packaging, security, or release defect; identify the first failed boundary before changing code. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in installable reproducible Bookbot package and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Separate import package, tests, fixtures, documentation, and build artifacts with a src layout and explicit package discovery.
Declare canonical name, version policy, Python requirement, description, license, authorship, classifiers, URLs, and build system in pyproject.toml.
Support python -m execution and an installed console script that call the same testable main boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Installed Smoke
steps.instruction:
Order the Installed Smoke Bookbot and Python corpus-to-release evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Installed Smoke.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Installed Smoke assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Bookbot and Python Order the regression proof · Debug Installed Smoke, keep Installed Smoke connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Bookbot team handles case 2798 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to build inspected wheel and source artifacts from an identified revision and test a fresh installation; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Install the built artifact into a fresh environment and test help, success, changed input, failure, and version output.

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Install the built artifact into a fresh environment and test help, success, changed input, failure, and version output.

options.text: For installed smoke · fault isolation, run one convenient ASCII text, omit corpus authority, repository and policy identity, bytes, encoding, Unicode, offsets, reconciliation, ties, stderr, status, changed cases, tests, recovery, and native transfer, then accept a plausible report as Bookbot proof. This does not satisfy Installed Smoke for installable reproducible Bookbot package.
A minimal trace that preserves the preconditions, contradicts the misconception, and satisfies Installed Smoke.
Another successful Installed Smoke run of the same installable reproducible Bookbot package happy path. That claim fails the installed smoke · fault isolation evidence boundary for Installed Smoke.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Installed Smoke
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-installed-smoke-15t8f3" that uses case 6752 to build inspected wheel and source artifacts from an identified revision and test a fresh installation. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Installed Smoke.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Installed Smoke into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Installed Smoke, keep Installed Smoke connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-installed-smoke-15t8f3
# Competency: bookbot-installed-smoke.
# Case 6752: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable.
def evidence_bookbot_installed_smoke_15t8f3(metadata_fields: int = 9, artifact_count: int = 2, clean_revision: bool = True, contents_inspected: bool = True, fresh_install_passed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_15t8f3 = "15t8f3-3f8t51-6752"
    assert evidence_variant_15t8f3
    evidence_axes_15t8f3 = (
        "corpus15t8f3",
        "policy3f8t51",
        "revision6752",
        "command15t8f36752",
        "report3f8t516752",
        "test675215t8f3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_15t8f3)) == 6
    if metadata_fields < 6 or artifact_count < 2:
        return False, artifact_count, "package-shape"
    if not clean_revision:
        return False, artifact_count, "dirty-build"
    if not contents_inspected:
        return False, artifact_count, "unknown-contents"
    if not fresh_install_passed:
        return False, artifact_count, "install-failure"
    assert artifact_count >= 2
    return True, metadata_fields, "installable-artifacts"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Installed Smoke structural check is only one gate. installable reproducible Bookbot package must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Installed Smoke, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Installed Smoke precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Installed Smoke, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A debug Bookbot team handles case 9849 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to build inspected wheel and source artifacts from an identified revision and test a fresh installation; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Install the built artifact into a fresh environment and test help, success, changed input, failure, and version output.

For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Installed Smoke, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Tests run from the source checkout prove the wheel is installable.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Install the built artifact into a fresh environment and test help, success, changed input, failure, and version output.
